forecast-period.text: A high pressure system over Victoria extends a ridge over South Australia, with the ridge expected to weaken during Tuesday. A trough will develop in the west on Tuesday, ahead of a weak cold front that is expected to extend over southern parts during Wednesday. Another ridge will extend over the west of the state later Wednesday, with a high centre expected to build over the Bight Thursday and Friday.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.

Areas of morning fog on and east of the ranges. A dry day before showers develop about the coasts during the evening. Maximum temperatures slightly above average. North to northwesterly winds, shifting southwest to southerly in the west during the afternoon and evening.

Isolated showers extending through the agricultural districts during the morning, before mostly contracting to coastal areas in the evening. Small hail about the southeast coast during the evening. Dry otherwise. Maximum temperatures above average about pastoral districts, tending slightly below average in the agricultural districts. Southwest to southerly winds, fresh at times about the southeast coast.

Isolated showers about the southeast and coastal areas, with small hail about the southeast coast during the morning. Showers easing later in the day. Dry elsewhere. Maximum temperatures generally below average. Southwest to southerly winds, fresh at times about the southeast coast and the pastoral districts.

text.p: Isolated light showers about the southeast and coastal areas. Dry otherwise. Maximum temperatures below average. Light winds in the south. Southeasterly winds over the pastoral districts, fresh at times in the far north.

Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Friday: Mostly dry until Wednesday, then totals of generally less than 5 mm possible about southern agricultural area and less than 2 mm possible about northern agricultural areas.
